Side_Effect: Hot flashes, decreased libido, injection site reaction. Serious: Initial 'flare' in cancer symptoms, decreased bone mineral density.
Dosage: Subcutaneous depot injection (3.6 mg every 28 days or 10.8 mg every 12 weeks).

Symptom_1: Prevention and treatment of Chemotherapy-Induced Nausea and Vomiting (CINV)
Symptom_2: Prevention and treatment of Postoperative Nausea and Vomiting (PONV)
Side_Effect: Headache, constipation, asthenia (weakness).
Dosage: Oral: 1 mg twice daily. IV: 1 mg to 2 mg infusion.

Symptom_1: Treatment of systemic Dermatophyte Fungal Infections (Tinea capitis/pedis)
Side_Effect: Headache, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, photosensitivity (sun sensitivity). Serious: Hepatotoxicity, allergic reactions (skin).
Dosage: 500 mg to 1000 mg daily in divided doses with food.

Symptom_1: Severe refractory Hypertension (rarely used due to side effects)
Side_Effect: Postural hypotension (severe), dizziness, diarrhea, fluid retention.
Dosage: Initial: 10 mg to 12.5 mg once daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of acute and chronic Schizophrenia
Side_Effect: Extrapyramidal symptoms (acute dystonia, tardive dyskinesia), sedation, QT prolongation.
Dosage: Oral: 0.5 mg to 10 mg twice daily. IM (acute): 2 mg to 5 mg. Decanoate: Monthly injection.

Symptom_1: Treatment of acute DVT/PE (Deep Vein Thrombosis/Pulmonary Embolism)
Side_Effect: Bleeding (major or minor). Serious: Heparin-induced Thrombocytopenia (HIT - requires immediate discontinuation).
Dosage: IV infusion (Dosing based on weight/PTT monitoring). Prophylaxis: 5,000 units SC twice daily.

Symptom_1: Management of severe Hypertension (Hypertensive crisis, often in pregnancy)
Side_Effect: Headache, flushing, palpitations, tachycardia. Serious: Drug-induced Lupus Syndrome (dose and time-dependent).
Dosage: 25 mg to 100 mg twice to four times daily. IV dose for emergency.

Symptom_1: Relief of inflammation, itching, and pain associated with various Skin Conditions
Side_Effect: Skin thinning, burning, itching (topical). Glucocorticoid side effects (oral).
Dosage: Topical: Apply 1-4 times daily. Oral: 20 mg to 30 mg daily (divided doses) for replacement.

Symptom_1: Treatment of acute, severe conditions (e.g., Anaphylaxis, severe Asthma, Adrenal crisis)
Side_Effect: Fluid retention, mood changes, hyperglycemia. Serious: Adrenal suppression (long-term use).
Dosage: IV/IM injection (100 mg to 500 mg dose is common).

Symptom_1: Management of moderate to severe Pain
Side_Effect: Constipation, nausea, vomiting, dizziness, sedation. Serious: Respiratory Depression, addiction/dependence.
Dosage: Oral: 2 mg to 8 mg every 3-4 hours. IV: 0.2 mg to 1 mg every 3-4 hours.

Symptom_1: Management of Sickle Cell Disease (reduces crisis frequency)
Side_Effect: Myelosuppression (dose-limiting: low WBC/platelets), GI upset, skin ulcers.
Dosage: Sickle Cell: 15 $\text{mg}/\text{kg}/\text{day}$ (titrated). CML: 20-30 $\text{mg}/\text{kg}/\text{day}$.

Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, headache, rash. Serious: Retinal toxicity (vision damage, requires regular ophthalmological exams), QT prolongation.
Dosage: 200 mg to 400 mg once daily.

Side_Effect: Drowsiness, dry mouth, headache. Serious: QT prolongation (higher doses), risk of paradoxical CNS stimulation in children.
Dosage: Anxiety: 50 mg to 100 mg 4 times daily. Pruritus: 25 mg 3-4 times daily.

Symptom_2: Edema associated with Congestive Heart Failure
Side_Effect: Low potassium (hypokalemia), dizziness, headache, fatigue.
Dosage: 1.5 mg MR or 2.5 mg once daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of HIV-1 Infection (part of combination therapy)
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal pain, kidney stones (nephrolithiasis).
Dosage: 800 mg every 8 hours (usually boosted with Ritonavir).

Symptom_1: Moderate to severe Rheumatoid Arthritis and Osteoarthritis
Side_Effect: Headache (frontal), dizziness, vertigo, GI upset. Serious: GI bleeding, CNS effects, renal damage.
Dosage: 25 mg to 50 mg two to three times daily.

Side_Effect: Drowsiness, dizziness, fatigue, orthostatic hypotension, dry mouth.
Dosage: 20 mg twice daily.

Symptom_2: Crohn's Disease, Ulcerative Colitis, Psoriasis, Ankylosing Spondylitis
Side_Effect: Infusion reactions (fever, rash, headache), upper respiratory tract infections. Serious: Serious infections (TB, Sepsis), heart failure, lymphoma.
Price: Extremely High Cost; Specialty IV Infusion
Dosage: IV Infusion (Dosing complex, weight-based, typically every 8 weeks).

Symptom_1: Adjunctive therapy for some Viral Infections (Herpes, Subacute Sclerosing Panencephalitis - SSPE)
Side_Effect: Increased serum and urine uric acid, joint pain, fatigue, headache.
Dosage: 1 g three to four times daily.

Symptom_1: Peripheral Vascular Disease (used to treat intermittent claudication)
Side_Effect: Flushing, itching, gastrointestinal disturbances.
Dosage: 500 mg to 1 g three times daily.

Side_Effect: Hypoglycemia (low blood sugar), lipodystrophy (changes at injection site), weight gain.
Dosage: Subcutaneous injection (Dosing highly individualized, multiple times daily pre-meal).

Symptom_1: Hepatitis C and B (now largely replaced by DAA)
Symptom_2: Various Cancers (Hairy Cell Leukemia, Malignant Melanoma)
Side_Effect: Flu-like symptoms (fever, fatigue, muscle aches), headache, nausea, depression, myelosuppression.
Dosage: SC or IM injection (Dosing highly variable, based on indication).

Symptom_1: Hepatitis C and B (now largely replaced by DAA)
Symptom_2: Various Cancers (Follicular Lymphoma, AIDS-related Kaposi's Sarcoma)
Side_Effect: Flu-like symptoms (fever, fatigue, muscle aches), headache, depression, myelosuppression.
Dosage: SC or IM injection (Dosing highly variable).

Symptom_1: Relapsing Forms of Multiple Sclerosis (MS) (Reduces relapse rate)
Side_Effect: Flu-like symptoms, injection site reactions, fever, headache. Serious: Depression, suicidal ideation, hepatotoxicity.
Dosage: SC or IM injection (Dosing schedule varies by product, e.g., 3 times weekly, every other day).

Symptom_1: Reduction of frequency/severity of severe infections in Chronic Granulomatous Disease (CGD)
Side_Effect: Flu-like symptoms, fever, headache, injection site reactions, rash.
Dosage: 50 $\mu\text{g}/\text{m}^2$ SC three times weekly.

Symptom_1: Treatment of unresectable or metastatic Melanoma
Side_Effect: Fatigue, diarrhea, pruritus, rash, colitis. Serious: Immune-related adverse events (irAEs) affecting colon, liver, skin, endocrine glands.
Price: Extremely High Cost; Oncology IV use only
Dosage: IV infusion (Dosing complex, based on weight/protocol).

Symptom_1: Maintenance treatment of COPD (Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease)
Side_Effect: Dry mouth, cough, headache, pharyngitis, nausea. Contraindicated: Narrow-angle glaucoma.
Dosage: 2-3 puffs 3-4 times daily via inhaler. Nebulized solution for acute exacerbations.

Side_Effect: Dizziness, headache, fatigue. Serious: Angioedema, hyperkalemia, renal impairment.
Dosage: 150 mg once daily (max 300 mg daily).

Side_Effect: Severe diarrhea (early and delayed), myelosuppression (neutropenia), nausea, vomiting, alopecia.
Price: Very High Cost; Oncology IV use only
Dosage: IV infusion (Dosing complex, based on $\text{m}^2$ and protocol).

Symptom_1: Treatment of severe Iron Deficiency Anemia (where oral iron is inappropriate)
Side_Effect: Allergic reactions (including anaphylaxis), hypotension, headache, fever, arthralgia.
Dosage: IV infusion (Test dose usually required; total dose calculated based on body weight/hemoglobin).

Symptom_1: Treatment of Iron Deficiency Anemia (when oral iron is ineffective/intolerated)
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, headache, dizziness, injection site reactions. Serious: Hypersensitivity reactions (anaphylaxis).
Price: Very High Cost; Hospital/IV use only
Dosage: IV infusion (Total calculated iron dose, often 1000 mg in one infusion, repeated if needed).

Symptom_1: Treatment of Iron Deficiency Anemia (especially in Chronic Kidney Disease patients)
Side_Effect: Transient taste disturbance (metallic taste), hypotension (if infused too fast), injection site reactions. Serious: Hypersensitivity reactions (anaphylaxis).
Dosage: IV infusion (Dosing complex, often 100 mg to 300 mg per dose, repeated several times).

Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, headache, hypokalemia. Serious: Hepatotoxicity, infusion-related reactions.
Dosage: Loading dose: 200 mg every 8 hours for 6 doses. Maintenance: 200 mg once daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment and prevention of Tuberculosis (TB) (always used in combination for active TB)
Side_Effect: Peripheral neuropathy (preventable with Pyridoxine), hepatotoxicity, rash, fever.
Dosage: Active TB: 5 $\text{mg}/\text{kg}$ to 10 $\text{mg}/\text{kg}$ daily. Prophylaxis: 300 mg daily for 6-9 months.

Symptom_1: Acute and chronic prophylaxis of Angina Pectoris
Side_Effect: Headache (severe, common), flushing, dizziness, hypotension.
Dosage: 5 mg to 40 mg two to three times daily. Sublingual for acute angina.

Symptom_1: Prophylaxis against chronic stable Angina Pectoris
Side_Effect: Headache (dose-related), flushing, dizziness, hypotension. Must have a daily nitrate-free interval to prevent tolerance.
Dosage: 20 mg twice daily, or 60 mg MR once daily (with a break period).

Side_Effect: Dry skin/lips/eyes, nosebleeds, joint pain. Serious: Teratogenic (causes severe birth defects - mandatory pregnancy prevention programs), depression, elevated triglycerides/liver enzymes.
Dosage: 0.5 $\text{mg}/\text{kg}/\text{day}$ to 1 $\text{mg}/\text{kg}/\text{day}$ in two divided doses for 15-20 weeks.

Symptom_2: Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS), maintenance of soft stools (e.g., hemorrhoids)
Side_Effect: Bloating, flatulence, abdominal discomfort. Requires adequate fluid intake to prevent obstruction.
Dosage: 1-2 teaspoons (or one sachet) in a full glass of water, 1-3 times daily.

Side_Effect: Headache, flushing, dizziness, peripheral edema.
Dosage: 2.5 mg twice daily (Max 10 mg daily).

Symptom_1: Treatment of systemic Fungal Infections (Histoplasmosis, Blastomycosis)
Side_Effect: Nausea, diarrhea, abdominal pain, headache. Serious: Dose-related negative inotropy (heart failure risk), hepatotoxicity.
Dosage: 100 mg to 400 mg daily in divided doses, or pulse dosing for onychomycosis.

Symptom_1: Treatment of chronic stable Angina (in patients with normal sinus rhythm)
Symptom_2: Chronic Heart Failure (NYHA Class II-IV, in sinus rhythm)
Side_Effect: Luminous phenomena (phosphenes/visual disturbance), bradycardia (slow heart rate), atrial fibrillation.
Dosage: 5 mg twice daily (Max 7.5 mg twice daily).

Symptom_1: Treatment of Multiple Myeloma (in combination with other agents)
Side_Effect: Diarrhea, constipation, peripheral neuropathy, nausea, vomiting, thrombocytopenia.
Dosage: 4 mg once weekly (Days 1, 8, and 15) for 3 weeks of a 28-day cycle.

Symptom_2: Sedation/Analgesia (Procedural Sedation), Severe Treatment-Resistant Depression (low dose)
Side_Effect: Vivid dreams, emergence reactions (delirium, hallucinations), hypertension, tachycardia, increased intracranial pressure.
Dosage: Anesthesia: 1-4 $\text{mg}/\text{kg}$ IV or 6.5-13 $\text{mg}/\text{kg}$ IM. Analgesia/Depression: Sub-anesthetic doses.

Symptom_1: Treatment of systemic Fungal Infections (now rarely used orally)
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, pruritus. Serious: Severe Hepatotoxicity (Black Box Warning), QT prolongation, adrenal insufficiency.
Dosage: Oral: 200 mg once daily. Topical: Apply to affected area 1-2 times daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of Rheumatoid Arthritis and Osteoarthritis
Symptom_2: Mild to moderate pain, Dysmenorrhea (menstrual cramps)
Side_Effect: Dyspepsia, nausea, abdominal pain, headache. Serious: GI bleeding, renal toxicity, increased cardiovascular risk.
Dosage: 50 mg to 100 mg two to three times daily.

Symptom_1: Short-term management of acute, moderate to severe Pain
Side_Effect: Drowsiness, dizziness, nausea, headache. Serious: High risk of GI bleeding/ulceration (short-term use only), renal failure.
Dosage: Oral: 10 mg every 4-6 hours (Max 40 mg/day, total duration $\le$ 5 days). IV: 15-30 mg every 6 hours.

Side_Effect: Drowsiness, dry mouth, dizziness, weight gain.
Dosage: Oral: 1 mg twice daily. Eye drops: 1 drop twice daily.

Symptom_1: Bowel cleansing before Colonoscopy or surgery
Symptom_2: Treatment of severe Chronic Constipation/Fecal Impaction
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, abdominal fullness, bloating, electrolyte disturbance.
Dosage: Entire volume (4 liters) consumed over a few hours prior to procedure.

Symptom_1: Management of Hypertension (Chronic and Hypertensive Emergencies)
Side_Effect: Postural hypotension, dizziness, fatigue, scalp tingling. Serious: Bradycardia, heart failure, bronchospasm.
Dosage: Oral: 100 mg to 400 mg twice daily. IV infusion for emergency.

Side_Effect: Headache, flushing, dizziness, peripheral edema, palpitations.
Dosage: 4 mg once daily (Max 6 mg daily).

Side_Effect: Dizziness, headache, nausea, diplopia (double vision), blurred vision, ataxia. Serious: PR interval prolongation (ECG change), risk of syncope.
Dosage: Initial: 50 mg twice daily. Maintenance: 100 mg to 200 mg twice daily.

Symptom_2: Prevention and treatment of Hepatic Encephalopathy
Side_Effect: Flatulence, abdominal distension, nausea, diarrhea (high dose).
Dosage: Constipation: 15-45 $\text{mL}$ once or twice daily. Hepatic Encephalopathy: 30-45 $\text{mL}$ three to four times daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of chronic Hepatitis B Virus (HBV) infection
Symptom_2: Treatment of HIV-1 Infection (part of combination therapy)
Side_Effect: Headache, nausea, diarrhea, fatigue. Serious: Lactic acidosis, severe hepatomegaly with steatosis (rare).
Dosage: HBV: 100 mg once daily. HIV: 150 mg twice daily or 300 mg once daily (in combination).

Side_Effect: Dizziness, ataxia, somnolence, headache, diplopia. Serious: Stevens-Johnson Syndrome (SJS)/Toxic Epidermal Necrolysis (TEN) - requires slow titration.
Dosage: Highly complex titration schedule, maintenance typically 100 mg to 500 mg daily.

Symptom_2: Treatment of symptoms associated with neuroendocrine tumors (NETs)
Side_Effect: Diarrhea, abdominal pain, nausea, gallstones, injection site reactions.
Dosage: Deep SC injection (60 mg, 90 mg, or 120 mg) every 4 weeks.

Symptom_1: Treatment and maintenance of Peptic Ulcers (Gastric/Duodenal)
Side_Effect: Headache, diarrhea, abdominal pain, nausea. Long-term risk: $\text{C}$. difficile infection, low magnesium, bone fracture.
Dosage: 15 mg to 30 mg once daily, 30 minutes before food.

Symptom_1: Reduction of high serum Phosphate levels in Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) patients on dialysis
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, constipation, abdominal pain.
Dosage: 500 mg to 1000 mg (or more) taken with each meal.

Symptom_1: $\text{HER2}$-positive Breast Cancer (in combination with other agents)
Side_Effect: Diarrhea, rash, nausea, vomiting, fatigue. Serious: Hepatotoxicity, decreased LVEF (heart function).
Price: Extremely High Cost; Oncology use only
Dosage: 1250 mg once daily (continuously) in combination with capecitabine.

Symptom_1: Treatment of moderate to severe active Rheumatoid Arthritis (RA)
Side_Effect: Diarrhea, nausea, rash, alopecia (hair loss), headache. Serious: Hepatotoxicity (liver damage), Teratogenic (Category X - pregnancy contraindication).
Dosage: Initial loading dose (100 mg $\times$ 3 days) followed by 10 mg or 20 mg once daily.

Side_Effect: Diarrhea, rash, fatigue, nausea, peripheral neuropathy. Serious: Deep Vein Thrombosis/Pulmonary Embolism (DVT/PE - requires prophylaxis), neutropenia, teratogenic (severe birth defects).
Price: Extremely High Cost; Strict dispensing/monitoring program
Dosage: 10 mg to 25 mg daily (cycled with days off).

Symptom_1: Management of Neutropenia (low white blood cells) following chemotherapy
Symptom_2: Mobilization of hematopoietic stem cells for transplantation
Side_Effect: Bone pain (common), injection site reactions, headache, fatigue. Serious: Splenic rupture, Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome (ARDS).
Dosage: 5 $\mu\text{g}/\text{kg}$ to 10 $\mu\text{g}/\text{kg}$ SC once daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of progressive, differentiated Thyroid Cancer (radioiodine refractory)
Symptom_2: Advanced Renal Cell Carcinoma (RCC), Hepatocellular Carcinoma (HCC)
Side_Effect: Hypertension, fatigue, diarrhea, decreased appetite, proteinuria, weight loss. Serious: Hemorrhage, arterial thromboembolism, hepatotoxicity.
Price: Extremely High Cost; Oncology use only
Dosage: 10 mg to 24 mg once daily.

Side_Effect: Headache, flushing, peripheral edema, dizziness, palpitations.
Dosage: 10 mg once daily (Max 20 mg daily).

Symptom_1: Adjuvant treatment of Estrogen-receptor positive Breast Cancer (postmenopausal women)
Side_Effect: Hot flashes, arthralgia (joint pain), night sweats, fatigue, headache, hypercholesterolemia. Serious: Increased fracture risk, thromboembolism.
Price: Very High Cost; Oncology use only
Dosage: 2.5 mg once daily.

Symptom_2: Endometriosis, Uterine Fibroids, Central Precocious Puberty
Side_Effect: Hot flashes, decreased libido, injection site reaction. Serious: Initial 'flare' in cancer symptoms, decreased bone mineral density.
Dosage: Depot Injection (IM/SC) every 1, 3, 4, or 6 months (dose varies by formulation).

Symptom_1: Adjuvant therapy for Colorectal Cancer (historical use, unlicensed in many regions)
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal pain. Serious: Agranulocytosis (severe low white blood cells), neurotoxicity.
Dosage: Varies by indication.

Symptom_2: Myoclonic Seizures, Primary Generalized Tonic-Clonic Seizures
Side_Effect: Somnolence, dizziness, headache, behavioral changes (agitation, irritability, aggression).
Dosage: Initial: 500 mg twice daily. Maintenance: up to 1500 mg twice daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of seasonal and perennial Allergic Rhinitis
Side_Effect: Drowsiness, dry mouth, fatigue, headache. Less sedating than first-generation antihistamines.
Dosage: 5 mg once daily, usually in the evening.

Symptom_1: Treatment of various susceptible Bacterial Infections (e.g., Pneumonia, UTI, Skin/Soft Tissue)
Side_Effect: Nausea, diarrhea, headache, dizziness. Serious: Tendon rupture (Black Box Warning), QT prolongation, peripheral neuropathy, $\text{C}$. difficile infection.
Dosage: 250 mg to 750 mg once daily, depending on infection.

Symptom_2: Antidote for Folic acid antagonists (e.g., Pyrimethamine)
Side_Effect: Rare allergic reactions. This is the active isomer of Folinic Acid.
Dosage: Dosing varies widely (complex protocols for Methotrexate rescue).

Symptom_1: Management of acute and chronic Psychosis
Symptom_2: Sedation, relief of severe pain (palliative care/adjunct analgesic)
Side_Effect: Sedation (pronounced), orthostatic hypotension, dry mouth, blurred vision. Serious: QT prolongation, extrapyramidal symptoms.
Dosage: Oral: 25 mg to 50 mg three times daily (psychosis). Analgesia: 12.5 mg to 25 mg.

Symptom_2: Thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) suppression in thyroid cancer
Side_Effect: Tachycardia, palpitations, tremors, heat intolerance, weight loss (symptoms of hyperthyroidism/overdose).
Dosage: Highly individualized dose, typically 1.6 $\mu\text{g}/\text{kg}$ daily, taken 30-60 mins before breakfast.

Side_Effect: Dizziness, drowsiness, paresthesia, metallic taste. Serious: Seizures, cardiotoxicity (IV overdose/rapid infusion).
Dosage: Varies greatly by route/concentration (e.g., 1%-2% solution for local infiltration; IV bolus/infusion for arrhythmias).

Symptom_1: Treatment of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (improves glycemic control)
Side_Effect: Nasopharyngitis, headache, diarrhea. Rare: Pancreatitis, bullous pemphigoid, joint pain.
Dosage: 5 mg once daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of serious infections caused by Gram-Positive bacteria (e.g., $\text{VRE}$, $\text{MRSA}$ Pneumonia/Skin)
Side_Effect: Diarrhea, headache, nausea, elevated liver enzymes. Serious: Myelosuppression (thrombocytopenia), Serotonin Syndrome (with SSRIs), peripheral and optic neuropathy (long-term).
Dosage: 600 mg twice daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of severe Hypothyroidism (Myxedema Coma - IV use)
Symptom_2: Supplemental therapy for hypothyroidism (oral, usually adjunct to $\text{T}_4$)
Side_Effect: Tachycardia, palpitations, tremors, weight loss (symptoms of hyperthyroidism/overdose).
Dosage: Highly individualized dose, usually 25 $\mu\text{g}$ to 75 $\mu\text{g}$ daily (oral). IV for Myxedema Coma.

Symptom_1: Management of Neutropenia (low white blood cells) following chemotherapy
Side_Effect: Bone pain (common), headache, nausea. Serious: Splenic rupture, ARDS. Pegylated, so longer acting.
Dosage: 6 mg SC once per chemotherapy cycle.

Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, headache. Serious: Pancreatitis, risk of Medullary Thyroid Carcinoma (MTC) - in animal models.
Dosage: Initial: 0.6 mg SC daily. Titrated to 1.2 mg or 1.8 mg SC daily.

Side_Effect: Dry cough, dizziness, fatigue. Serious: Angioedema, hyperkalemia, renal impairment.
Dosage: 10 mg to 40 mg once daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of acute Mania and maintenance for Bipolar Disorder
Side_Effect: Tremor, polyuria (excessive urination)/polydipsia, weight gain, nausea, loose stools. Serious: Toxicity (narrow therapeutic range, symptoms include severe GI distress, ataxia, confusion), renal damage, hypothyroidism.
Dosage: Highly individualized dose (monitoring serum levels), typically 600 mg to 900 mg twice daily (MR).

Symptom_1: Treatment of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (as an adjunct to diet and exercise)
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, headache, diarrhea. Serious: Pancreatitis, severe hypersensitivity reactions.
Dosage: Initial: 10 $\mu\text{g}$ SC once daily for 14 days. Maintenance: 20 $\mu\text{g}$ SC once daily.

Side_Effect: Dry mouth, constipation, dizziness, blurred vision, sedation. Less cardiotoxic than other TCAs.
Dosage: 70 mg three times daily (Max 210 mg daily).

Symptom_1: Symptomatic relief of acute, non-specific Diarrhea
Side_Effect: Constipation, dizziness, nausea, abdominal cramps. Serious: Cardiac arrhythmias (high dose/abuse), paralytic ileus.
Dosage: Initial: 4 mg. Then 2 mg after each loose stool (Max 16 mg/day).

Symptom_1: Treatment of HIV-1 Infection (part of combination therapy)
Side_Effect: Diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, headache. Serious: PR/QT prolongation, hepatotoxicity, metabolic disturbances (dyslipidemia/hyperglycemia).
Dosage: Usually combined with Ritonavir (e.g., 400 $\text{mg}$ Lopinavir/100 $\text{mg}$ Ritonavir) twice daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of seasonal and perennial Allergic Rhinitis
Side_Effect: Headache, somnolence (mild), fatigue, dry mouth.
Dosage: 10 mg once daily.

Symptom_2: Treatment of Status Epilepticus (IV), Preoperative sedation
Side_Effect: Sedation, dizziness, weakness, unsteadiness, anterograde amnesia. Serious: Respiratory depression, physical/psychological dependence.
Dosage: Anxiety: 1 mg to 3 mg two to three times daily. Status Epilepticus: 2 $\text{mg}$ to 4 $\text{mg}$ IV.

Side_Effect: Dizziness, headache, upper respiratory infection. Serious: Angioedema, hyperkalemia, renal impairment.
Dosage: 50 mg to 100 mg once daily.

Side_Effect: Headache, GI upset, muscle pain (myalgia). Serious: Rhabdomyolysis (severe muscle breakdown), hepatotoxicity.
Dosage: 20 mg to 80 mg once daily with the evening meal.

Symptom_1: Treatment of acute and chronic Schizophrenia
Side_Effect: Sedation, dizziness, dry mouth, extrapyramidal symptoms. Serious: Neuroleptic Malignant Syndrome (NMS), seizures.
Dosage: 10 mg to 50 mg two to four times daily.

Side_Effect: Somnolence, akathisia (restlessness), nausea, weight gain (less than some others). Must be taken with food (at least 350 calories).
Dosage: 40 mg to 160 mg once daily with food.

Side_Effect: Headache, anemia, nasopharyngitis, bronchitis. Serious: Hepatotoxicity, peripheral edema. Teratogenic (causes birth defects).
Dosage: 10 mg once daily.

Side_Effect: Diarrhea, abdominal cramps (high dose).
Dosage: As directed for antacid use. Supplement dose varies.

Side_Effect: Constipation, diarrhea (less frequent than with magnesium-only).
Dosage: As directed for antacid use.

Symptom_1: Reduction of elevated Intracranial Pressure (ICP) and Intraocular Pressure
Symptom_2: Promotion of diuresis (increased urine flow) in renal failure
Side_Effect: Headache, nausea, vomiting, fluid/electrolyte imbalances, chest pain. Serious: Pulmonary edema, hypernatremia.
Dosage: IV infusion (Dose based on weight and required osmolarity).

Symptom_1: Treatment of HIV-1 Infection (specifically CCR5-tropic strain)
Side_Effect: Cough, upper respiratory infection, dizziness, hepatotoxicity, rash. Requires tropism testing before use.
Dosage: 300 mg twice daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of intestinal Worm Infections (Pinworm, Roundworm, Hookworm)
Side_Effect: Abdominal pain, diarrhea, elevated liver enzymes (high dose).
Dosage: Pinworm: 100 mg single dose, repeated in 2 weeks. Other worms: 100 mg twice daily for 3 days.

Symptom_1: Symptomatic relief of Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS) (pain, cramping)
Side_Effect: Indigestion, heartburn, headache, dizziness.
Dosage: 135 mg three times daily (standard) or 200 mg SR twice daily.

Symptom_1: Prevention and treatment of Motion Sickness
Side_Effect: Drowsiness, dry mouth, fatigue.
Dosage: Motion Sickness: 25 mg 1 hour before travel. Vertigo: 25 mg to 100 mg daily.

Symptom_2: Treatment of Endometriosis, Abnormal Uterine Bleeding, Adjunct to Estrogen Replacement
Side_Effect: Weight gain, headache, mood changes, irregular bleeding. Serious: Decreased bone mineral density (with long-term use).
Dosage: Contraception: 150 mg IM every 3 months. Oral: 5 mg to 10 mg daily.

Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, dizziness, insomnia, strange dreams. Serious: Neuropsychiatric reactions (anxiety, depression, psychosis, seizures) - Black Box Warning.
Dosage: Prophylaxis: 250 mg once weekly. Treatment: 750 mg then 500 mg 6-12 hours later.

Symptom_1: Treatment of mild to moderate Pain
Side_Effect: Diarrhea, abdominal pain, nausea, dizziness. Serious: GI bleeding, renal toxicity, liver impairment.
Dosage: 500 mg loading dose, then 250 mg every 6 hours (Max 7 days).

Symptom_1: Treatment of advanced Breast Cancer and Endometrial Cancer
Symptom_2: Appetite stimulation and weight gain (in cachexia due to AIDS/cancer)
Side_Effect: Weight gain, fluid retention, nausea, hot flashes. Serious: Thromboembolism, adrenal suppression.
Dosage: Cancer: 40 mg to 160 mg four times daily. Appetite: 400 mg to 800 mg once daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of Insomnia (especially primary insomnia in elderly)
Symptom_2: Circadian rhythm sleep disorders (e.g., Jet lag)
Side_Effect: Drowsiness, headache, dizziness, nausea.
Dosage: 1 mg to 5 mg (or up to 10 mg) 30 minutes before bedtime.

Symptom_1: Treatment of Osteoarthritis and Rheumatoid Arthritis
Side_Effect: Dyspepsia, abdominal pain, nausea, diarrhea. Less GI risk than non-selective NSAIDs (at lower doses). Serious: GI bleeding, cardiovascular risk (MI/Stroke), renal damage.
Dosage: 7.5 mg to 15 mg once daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of moderate to severe Alzheimer's Disease
Side_Effect: Dizziness, headache, confusion, constipation.
Dosage: Initial: 5 mg once daily. Titrated up to 10 mg twice daily (or 28 mg XR).

Symptom_1: Treatment of Infertility (to stimulate ovarian follicular growth or spermatogenesis)
Side_Effect: Ovarian Hyperstimulation Syndrome (OHSS), abdominal pain, injection site reactions, headache.
Dosage: Highly individualized SC/IM injection regimen, based on ovarian response monitoring.

Symptom_1: Treatment of severe susceptible Bacterial Infections (e.g., Meningitis, intra-abdominal infections)
Side_Effect: Diarrhea, nausea, headache, rash, inflammation at injection site. Serious: Seizures (rare), $\text{C}$. difficile infection.
Price: Extremely High Cost; Hospital/IV use only
Dosage: IV infusion (500 $\text{mg}$ to 2 $\text{g}$ every 8 hours, depending on infection/renal function).

Symptom_1: Treatment and maintenance of mild to moderate Ulcerative Colitis (UC)
Symptom_2: Maintenance of Crohn's Disease remission (less effective)
Side_Effect: Headache, nausea, abdominal pain, diarrhea. Serious: Interstitial Nephritis, fever, rash.
Dosage: Oral: 1.2 $\text{g}$ to 4.8 $\text{g}$ daily (divided doses). Rectal: Suppository/Enema once or twice daily.

Side_Effect: Diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, flatulence, abdominal discomfort. Serious: Lactic Acidosis (rare, but serious - especially with renal impairment), $\text{B}_{12}$ deficiency.
Dosage: Initial: 500 mg once or twice daily. Maintenance: 1 $\text{g}$ to 2 $\text{g}$ daily (Max 2.55 $\text{g}$ daily).

Side_Effect: Sedation, dizziness, nausea, vomiting, constipation. Serious: Respiratory Depression (risk of overdose), QT prolongation, physical dependence.
Uses: Synthetic Opioid Analgesic ($\text{NMDA}$ Antagonist/Serotonin-Norepinephrine Reuptake Inhibitor)
Dosage: Pain: 5 mg to 20 mg every 8-12 hours. Maintenance: Dosing highly individualized, usually 40 $\text{mg}$ to 120 $\text{mg}$ once daily.

Side_Effect: Rash, pruritus, hair loss, GI upset. Serious: Agranulocytosis (severe low white blood cells), hepatotoxicity (less common than Propylthiouracil).
Dosage: Initial: 10 mg to 30 mg daily (divided doses). Maintenance: 5 mg to 15 mg daily.

Symptom_2: Promotion of Hair Growth (Topical for Androgenetic Alopecia)
Side_Effect: Fluid retention, tachycardia, headache, hirsutism (Oral). Local irritation, itching (Topical).
Price: Oral: High Cost. Topical: ₹300 - ₹800 per bottle (2%/5%)
Dosage: Oral: 5 $\text{mg}$ to 40 $\text{mg}$ daily (always with a beta-blocker/diuretic). Topical: 1 $\text{mL}$ of 2% or 5% solution twice daily.

Side_Effect: Increased appetite, Weight gain (pronounced), sedation, dry mouth, dizziness. Less sexual dysfunction than SSRIs.
Dosage: Initial: 15 $\text{mg}$ once daily (usually at bedtime). Maintenance: 15 $\text{mg}$ to 45 $\text{mg}$ daily.

Symptom_2: Medical termination of pregnancy, Cervical ripening (induction of labor)
Side_Effect: Diarrhea, abdominal pain, nausea, headache. Serious: Abortifacient (Absolute Contraindication in pregnancy for ulcer prevention).
Dosage: Ulcer Prophylaxis: 200 $\mu\text{g}$ four times daily with food.

Side_Effect: Dizziness, insomnia, nausea, dry mouth, headache. Safer in terms of dietary restrictions than older MAOIs.
Dosage: 150 $\text{mg}$ to 300 $\text{mg}$ twice or three times daily (Max 600 $\text{mg}$).

Side_Effect: Headache, nausea, nervousness, insomnia, anxiety, dry mouth. Serious: Severe rash (SJS/TEN), Tachycardia/Hypertension.
Dosage: 200 $\text{mg}$ once daily (in the morning).

Symptom_1: Treatment of mild-to-moderate COVID-19 in high-risk adults
Side_Effect: Diarrhea, nausea, dizziness. Not recommended in pregnancy (animal toxicity data).
Dosage: 800 $\text{mg}$ twice daily for 5 days.

Symptom_1: Prophylaxis and chronic treatment of Asthma
Symptom_2: Relief of symptoms of Seasonal Allergic Rhinitis (SAR)
Side_Effect: Headache, GI upset, $\text{URI}$ symptoms. Serious: Neuropsychiatric events (e.g., aggression, depression, suicidal thoughts) - Black Box Warning.
Dosage: Adults: 10 $\text{mg}$ once daily (in the evening). Children: 4 $\text{mg}$ or 5 $\text{mg}$ once daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of symptoms of seasonal and perennial Allergic Rhinitis (Nasal Spray)
Symptom_2: Treatment of inflammatory and pruritic skin conditions (Topical)
Side_Effect: Headache, viral $\text{URI}$, pharyngitis (Nasal). Local skin atrophy, burning/stinging (Topical).
Dosage: Nasal: 2 sprays in each nostril once daily. Topical: Apply once daily.

Symptom_1: Management of severe acute and chronic Pain
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, constipation, drowsiness. Serious: Respiratory Depression, risk of dependence and addiction.
Dosage: Highly individualized. IR: 5 $\text{mg}$ to 30 $\text{mg}$ every 4 hours. ER: 15 $\text{mg}$ to 200 $\text{mg}$ every 8-24 hours.

Symptom_1: Treatment of various susceptible Bacterial Infections (e.g., Community-Acquired Pneumonia, Skin infections)
Side_Effect: Nausea, diarrhea, dizziness, headache. Serious: $\text{QT}$ prolongation, Tendon rupture (Black Box Warning), $\text{C}$. difficile infection, peripheral neuropathy.
Dosage: 400 $\text{mg}$ once daily.

Symptom_1: Topical treatment of susceptible skin infections (e.g., Impetigo)
Symptom_2: Eradication of nasal colonization of $\text{MRSA}$ (Nasal ointment)
Side_Effect: Local burning, stinging, itching.
Dosage: Topical: Apply three times daily for 3 to 5 days. Nasal: Apply twice daily for 5 days.

Symptom_1: Prevention of rejection in solid organ Transplantation (e.g., Kidney, Heart, Liver)
Side_Effect: Diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, leukopenia, anemia, infection. Serious: Increased risk of Lymphoma and skin malignancies, $\text{Pure Red Cell Aplasia}$. Teratogenic.
Dosage: Kidney Transplant: 1 $\text{g}$ twice daily. Heart/Liver: 1.5 $\text{g}$ twice daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of chemotherapy-induced Nausea and Vomiting (when other agents fail)
Side_Effect: Drowsiness, dizziness, dry mouth, euphoria, orthostatic hypotension. Serious: Psychosis, Tachycardia.
Dosage: 1 $\text{mg}$ to 2 $\text{mg}$ twice daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of Hypertension and chronic stable Angina
Symptom_2: Prophylaxis of Migraine, Management of Tremor (Off-label)
Side_Effect: Bradycardia, fatigue, dizziness, cold extremities.
Dosage: 40 $\text{mg}$ once daily (Max 320 $\text{mg}$).

Symptom_1: Treatment of serious susceptible Staphylococcal Infections (e.g., $\text{MSSA}$ Endocarditis)
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, rash, phlebitis (IV site). Serious: Neutropenia, hepatotoxicity.
Dosage: IV infusion (1 $\text{g}$ to 2 $\text{g}$ every 4-6 hours).

Symptom_1: Reversal of acute Opioid Overdose (opioid-induced respiratory depression)
Side_Effect: Withdrawal symptoms (nausea, vomiting, restlessness, body aches), tachycardia, hypertension.
Price: Very High Cost; Emergency use only
Dosage: IV/IM/SC/Intranasal (0.4 $\text{mg}$ to 2 $\text{mg}$, repeated as needed).

Symptom_1: Prevention of relapse in Alcohol Dependence
Symptom_2: Opioid Use Disorder (to block effects of abused opioids)
Side_Effect: Nausea, headache, dizziness, injection site pain (with long-acting form). Serious: Hepatotoxicity, depression/suicidality.
Dosage: Oral: 50 $\text{mg}$ once daily. Extended-release injection: 380 $\text{mg}$ IM every 4 weeks.

Symptom_1: Treatment of mild to moderate Pain
Side_Effect: Dyspepsia, nausea, heartburn, abdominal pain. Serious: GI bleeding/ulceration, increased cardiovascular risk, renal impairment.
Dosage: 250 $\text{mg}$ to 500 $\text{mg}$ twice daily.

Symptom_1: Acute treatment of Migraine attacks (with or without aura)
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, dizziness, somnolence, fatigue. Less risk of recurrence than other triptans.
Dosage: 1 $\text{mg}$ to 2.5 $\text{mg}$ at onset (may repeat once after 4 hours).

Side_Effect: Headache, fatigue, arthralgia, $\text{URI}$ symptoms. Serious: Progressive Multifocal Leukoencephalopathy (PML) (requires strict monitoring program - $\text{TOUCH}$).
Price: Extremely High Cost; Specialty IV Infusion
Dosage: 300 $\text{mg}$ IV infusion every 4 weeks.

Symptom_1: Prophylaxis of mild to moderate Asthma
Side_Effect: Unpleasant taste, headache, cough, pharyngitis.
Dosage: Inhalation: 2 to 4 puffs two to four times daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of HIV-1 Infection (part of combination therapy)
Side_Effect: Diarrhea (major side effect), nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain. Serious: Hyperglycemia/Diabetes, dyslipidemia.
Dosage: 1250 $\text{mg}$ twice daily or 750 $\text{mg}$ three times daily (with food).

Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal cramps, excessive salivation, bradycardia. Always given with an anticholinergic (e.g., Atropine).
Dosage: Varies by indication (often 0.5 $\text{mg}$ to 2 $\text{mg}$ IV/IM for reversal).

Symptom_1: Treatment of HIV-1 Infection (part of combination therapy)
Side_Effect: Rash (can be severe SJS/TEN), fever, nausea, fatigue. Serious: Severe Hepatotoxicity (Black Box Warning), lipodystrophy.
Dosage: Initial: 200 $\text{mg}$ once daily for 14 days. Maintenance: 200 $\text{mg}$ twice daily.

Side_Effect: Headache, dizziness, flushing, peripheral edema, reflex tachycardia.
Dosage: Oral: 20 $\text{mg}$ to 40 $\text{mg}$ three times daily. IV: Continuous infusion (titrated).

Symptom_1: Prophylaxis and treatment of chronic stable Angina Pectoris
Side_Effect: Headache (dose-related), flushing, dizziness. Serious: GI ulceration (rare, severe), hypotension.
Dosage: 10 $\text{mg}$ to 20 $\text{mg}$ twice daily.

Side_Effect: Headache, flushing, dizziness, peripheral edema.
Dosage: Varies by formulation; ER: 30 $\text{mg}$ to 90 $\text{mg}$ once daily.

Side_Effect: Rash, nausea, vomiting, headache, fatigue. Serious: $\text{QT}$ prolongation (requires ECG monitoring), hepatotoxicity, sudden death.
Price: Extremely High Cost; Oncology use only
Dosage: 300 $\text{mg}$ or 400 $\text{mg}$ twice daily (on an empty stomach).

Symptom_1: Prevention and treatment of neurological deficits due to vasospasm following Subarachnoid Hemorrhage (SAH)
Side_Effect: Hypotension, headache, dizziness, flushing.
Dosage: 60 $\text{mg}$ every 4 hours for 21 days (start within 4 days of $\text{SAH}$).

Side_Effect: Leukopenia/Thrombocytopenia, corneal opacity, conjunctivitis, rash. Requires a restricted diet (low in tyrosine and phenylalanine).
Dosage: Initial: 0.5 $\text{mg}/\text{kg}$ twice daily. Maintenance: Adjust to maintain serum succinylacetone levels.

Symptom_1: Treatment of active benign Gastric Ulcer
Side_Effect: Headache, dizziness, constipation, diarrhea.
Dosage: 150 $\text{mg}$ twice daily or 300 $\text{mg}$ at bedtime.

Symptom_1: Treatment of acute Hypotension (Low blood pressure) and Septic Shock
Side_Effect: Hypertension (high $\text{BP}$), headache, anxiety, bradycardia, extravasation (tissue damage at $\text{IV}$ site).
Dosage: IV infusion (0.01 $\text{mcg}/\text{kg}/\text{min}$ to 3 $\text{mcg}/\text{kg}/\text{min}$, titrated to effect).

Side_Effect: Dry mouth, blurred vision, constipation, dizziness, orthostatic hypotension. Less sedating than Amitriptyline.
Dosage: 25 $\text{mg}$ three to four times daily (or 75 $\text{mg}$ to 150 $\text{mg}$ once daily at night).

Symptom_1: Treatment of topical, intestinal, and vaginal Candidiasis (Yeast infection)
Symptom_2: Prevention of Candidiasis in immunocompromised patients
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, GI upset. Not absorbed systemically (very safe, but only works locally).
Dosage: Oral Suspension: 400,000 $\text{U}$ to 600,000 $\text{U}$ four times daily. Vaginal/Topical: Once or twice daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of Relapsing Forms of Multiple Sclerosis (MS)
Side_Effect: Infusion reactions (headache, fever, rash), upper respiratory tract infections. Serious: Increased risk of infection, malignancy.
Price: Extremely High Cost; Specialty IV Infusion
Dosage: 300 $\text{mg}$ IV initial dose, followed by 600 $\text{mg}$ IV every 6 months.

Symptom_1: Symptomatic treatment of Neuroendocrine Tumors (NETs) (Carcinoid syndrome, $\text{VIP}$omas)
Symptom_2: Treatment of Acromegaly, Bleeding Esophageal Varices
Side_Effect: Diarrhea, abdominal pain, nausea, gallstones, injection site reactions.
Dosage: SC: 50 $\mu\text{g}$ to 500 $\mu\text{g}$ three times daily. $\text{LAR}$ (long-acting): IM injection every 4 weeks.

Symptom_1: Treatment of various susceptible Bacterial Infections (e.g., $\text{UTI}$, Respiratory, Skin)
Side_Effect: Nausea, diarrhea, headache, dizziness. Serious: Tendon rupture, $\text{QT}$ prolongation, peripheral neuropathy.
Dosage: 200 $\text{mg}$ to 400 $\text{mg}$ twice daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of acute and chronic Schizophrenia
Symptom_2: Treatment of acute Mania and Bipolar Disorder (Maintenance)
Side_Effect: Weight gain (high risk), somnolence, dry mouth, dizziness, increased blood sugar/lipids. Serious: $\text{NMS}$, $\text{TD}$, $\text{QT}$ prolongation.
Dosage: Initial: 5 $\text{mg}$ to 10 $\text{mg}$ once daily. Maintenance: 10 $\text{mg}$ to 20 $\text{mg}$ daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of advanced Ovarian, Breast, Pancreatic, or Prostate Cancer ($\text{BRCA}$ mutation-associated)
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, fatigue, anemia, neutropenia. Serious: $\text{MDS}/\text{AML}$ (Myelodysplastic Syndrome/Acute Myeloid Leukemia).
Price: Extremely High Cost; Oncology use only
Dosage: 300 $\text{mg}$ twice daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of moderate to severe persistent Allergic Asthma
Side_Effect: Injection site reaction, viral infection, headache. Serious: Anaphylaxis (Black Box Warning, must be administered in a healthcare setting), malignancy risk (small, observed in clinical trials).
Price: Extremely High Cost; Specialty SC Injection
Dosage: SC injection every 2 or 4 weeks (dose and frequency based on weight and serum $\text{IgE}$ level).

Symptom_2: Treatment and prophylaxis of Peptic Ulcers, $\text{H}$. pylori eradication (in combination)
Side_Effect: Headache, diarrhea, nausea, abdominal pain. Long-term risk: $\text{C}$. difficile infection, low magnesium, bone fracture.
Dosage: 20 $\text{mg}$ to 40 $\text{mg}$ once daily, 30 minutes before food.

Symptom_1: Prevention and treatment of Chemotherapy-Induced Nausea and Vomiting (CINV)
Symptom_2: Prevention and treatment of Postoperative Nausea and Vomiting ($\text{PONV}$)
Side_Effect: Headache, constipation, malaise. Serious: $\text{QT}$ prolongation (dose-dependent), Serotonin Syndrome (rare).
Dosage: Oral: 8 $\text{mg}$ to 24 $\text{mg}$ (dose depends on risk). IV: 4 $\text{mg}$ to 16 $\text{mg}$ single dose.

Side_Effect: Varies (often compounded supplements).
Dosage: Varies widely.

Symptom_1: Prevention of severe Thrombocytopenia (low platelets) following chemotherapy
Side_Effect: Fluid retention, palpitations, tachycardia, conjunctival injection (red eyes). Serious: Congestive Heart Failure, Atrial Fibrillation.
Dosage: 50 $\mu\text{g}/\text{kg}$ SC once daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment and prophylaxis of Influenza A and B
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, headache. Serious: Neuropsychiatric events (especially in children), severe skin reactions.
Dosage: Treatment: 75 $\text{mg}$ twice daily for 5 days. Prophylaxis: 75 $\text{mg}$ once daily for 10 days.

Side_Effect: Peripheral sensory neuropathy (often cold-induced), nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, myelosuppression. Serious: Hypersensitivity reactions (infusion).
Price: Extremely High Cost; Oncology IV use only
Dosage: IV infusion (Dosing complex, typically 85 $\text{mg}/\text{m}^2$ every 2 weeks).

Side_Effect: Drowsiness, dizziness, unsteadiness, confusion. Shorter half-life than Diazepam.
Dosage: 10 $\text{mg}$ to 30 $\text{mg}$ three to four times daily.

Side_Effect: Dizziness, somnolence, headache, nausea, diplopia. Serious: Hyponatremia (low sodium), severe skin reactions (SJS/TEN).
Dosage: Initial: 300 $\text{mg}$ twice daily. Maintenance: 600 $\text{mg}$ to 1200 $\text{mg}$ daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of Overactive Bladder (OAB) (Urinary frequency, urgency, incontinence)
Side_Effect: Dry mouth, constipation, blurred vision, dizziness, somnolence. Serious: Heat prostration, urinary retention.
Dosage: 5 $\text{mg}$ two to three times daily (IR) or 5 $\text{mg}$ to 30 $\text{mg}$ once daily (ER).

Symptom_1: Management of moderate to severe Pain
Side_Effect: Constipation, nausea, vomiting, dizziness, sedation. Serious: Respiratory Depression, addiction/dependence.
Dosage: IR: 5 $\text{mg}$ to 15 $\text{mg}$ every 4-6 hours. CR: 10 $\text{mg}$ to 80 $\text{mg}$ twice daily.

Symptom_1: Symptomatic relief of Nasal Congestion (Colds, Allergies, Sinusitis)
Side_Effect: Local irritation, burning, sneezing. Serious: Rebound Congestion (Rhinitis Medicamentosa) with prolonged use ($\ge$ 3 days).
Dosage: 2 to 3 sprays in each nostril every 10-12 hours ($\le$ 3 days).

Symptom_1: Treatment of various susceptible Bacterial Infections (e.g., Acne, Respiratory, Urinary)
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, photosensitivity. Serious: Permanent tooth discoloration (in children < 8 years), pseudotumor cerebri.
Dosage: 250 $\text{mg}$ four times daily or 500 $\text{mg}$ twice daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of various Cancers (e.g., Ovarian, Breast, Non-Small Cell Lung)
Side_Effect: Neutropenia, peripheral neuropathy (dose-limiting), alopecia, joint/muscle pain. Serious: Hypersensitivity reactions (requires pre-medication).
Price: Extremely High Cost; Oncology IV use only
Dosage: IV infusion (Dosing complex, based on $\text{m}^2$ and protocol).

Symptom_1: Treatment of $\text{HR}$+ $\text{HER2}$- Advanced/Metastatic Breast Cancer (in combination with endocrine therapy)
Side_Effect: Neutropenia, leukopenia, fatigue, nausea, diarrhea. Requires dose interruption or reduction for neutropenia.
Price: Extremely High Cost; Oncology use only
Dosage: 125 $\text{mg}$ once daily for 21 days, followed by 7 days off (28-day cycle).

Symptom_1: Prophylaxis of serious illness caused by Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) in high-risk infants
Side_Effect: Fever, injection site reaction.
Dosage: 15 $\text{mg}/\text{kg}$ IM once monthly (during $\text{RSV}$ season, Max 5 doses).

Symptom_1: Prevention of acute and delayed Chemotherapy-Induced Nausea and Vomiting (CINV)
Symptom_2: Prevention of Postoperative Nausea and Vomiting ($\text{PONV}$)
Side_Effect: Headache, constipation. Longer half-life than first-generation $\text{5-HT}_3$ antagonists.
Dosage: 0.25 $\text{mg}$ IV single dose before chemotherapy.

Symptom_1: Symptomatic relief of premenstrual Bloating and Edema
Side_Effect: Dizziness, headache, nausea.
Dosage: 50 $\text{mg}$ (often combined with an analgesic) up to four times daily.

Symptom_2: Treatment of Paget's Disease of Bone, Metastatic Bone Disease
Side_Effect: Fever, fatigue, hypocalcemia (low calcium), bone pain. Serious: Renal toxicity, Osteonecrosis of the Jaw ($\text{ONJ}$).
Dosage: IV infusion (Dose based on indication, often 60 $\text{mg}$ to 90 $\text{mg}$).

Symptom_2: Treatment and prophylaxis of Peptic Ulcers, $\text{H}$. pylori eradication (in combination)
Side_Effect: Headache, diarrhea, flatulence, abdominal pain. Long-term risk: $\text{C}$. difficile infection, low magnesium, bone fracture.
Dosage: 20 $\text{mg}$ to 40 $\text{mg}$ once daily, 30 minutes before food.

Symptom_1: Management of mild to moderate Pain
Side_Effect: Rare allergic skin reactions. Serious: Hepatotoxicity (Liver damage) with overdose, especially when combined with alcohol.
Dosage: Adults: 500 $\text{mg}$ to 1000 $\text{mg}$ every 4-6 hours (Max 4 $\text{g}$/day).

Symptom_1: Short-term management of acute post-operative Pain
Side_Effect: Insomnia, dizziness, nausea, vomiting, injection site pain. Serious: Increased cardiovascular thrombotic risk (MI/Stroke), GI bleeding, anaphylaxis.
Price: Very High Cost; Hospital/IV use only
Dosage: 40 $\text{mg}$ IV/IM initially, followed by 20 $\text{mg}$ to 40 $\text{mg}$ every 6 to 12 hours.

Symptom_2: Treatment of Peptic Ulcers, $\text{H}$. pylori eradication (in combination)
Side_Effect: Headache, diarrhea, nausea, pharyngitis. Similar safety profile to other PPIs.
Dosage: 20 $\text{mg}$ once daily, 30 minutes before food.

Symptom_1: Treatment of chronic Hepatitis C Virus (HCV) infection (part of combination therapy)
Side_Effect: Fatigue, nausea, headache, insomnia. Requires co-administration with Ritonavir and other direct-acting antivirals ($\text{DAA}$).
Dosage: Usually combined in a fixed-dose tablet (e.g., Paritaprevir/Ritonavir/Ombitasvir).

Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, sexual dysfunction (high rate), somnolence, insomnia. Serious: Serotonin Syndrome, increased bleeding risk. Highest risk of withdrawal/discontinuation syndrome.
Dosage: 20 $\text{mg}$ once daily (Max 50 $\text{mg}$/day).

Symptom_1: Treatment of advanced Renal Cell Carcinoma (RCC)
Side_Effect: Diarrhea, hypertension, hair color change/depigmentation, fatigue, nausea, vomiting. Serious: Hepatotoxicity ($\text{Black Box Warning}$), thrombosis, hemorrhage, $\text{QT}$ prolongation.
Price: Extremely High Cost; Oncology use only
Dosage: 800 $\text{mg}$ once daily (on an empty stomach).

Symptom_1: Management of Neutropenia (low white blood cells) following chemotherapy
Side_Effect: Bone pain (common), injection site reactions, headache. Longer acting due to pegylation. Serious: Splenic rupture, $\text{ARDS}$.
Dosage: 6 $\text{mg}$ SC once per chemotherapy cycle.

Symptom_1: Treatment of Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (NSCLC)
Side_Effect: Myelosuppression (neutropenia, anemia), nausea, vomiting, fatigue. Requires folic acid and $\text{B}_{12}$ supplementation to mitigate toxicity.
Price: Extremely High Cost; Oncology IV use only
Dosage: IV infusion (Dosing complex, based on $\text{m}^2$ and protocol).

Symptom_2: Severe active Rheumatoid Arthritis (RA) (used less often now)
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, loss of taste (ageusia), rash. Serious: Myelosuppression, renal toxicity (proteinuria), severe hypersensitivity.
Dosage: Wilson's: 250 $\text{mg}$ to 500 $\text{mg}$ four times daily. RA: Slowly titrated up to 1 $\text{g}$ daily.

Symptom_1: Prophylaxis of chronic stable Angina Pectoris
Side_Effect: Headache, dizziness, flushing, hypotension.
Dosage: 10 $\text{mg}$ to 20 $\text{mg}$ three to four times daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment and prophylaxis of Pneumocystis Pneumonia (PCP)
Side_Effect: IV/IM: Hypotension, hypoglycemia/hyperglycemia, nephrotoxicity. Inhaled: Cough, bronchospasm, metallic taste.
Dosage: IV/IM: 4 $\text{mg}/\text{kg}$ once daily for 14-21 days (treatment). Inhaled: 300 $\text{mg}$ once every 4 weeks (prophylaxis).

Symptom_1: Management of moderate to severe Pain
Side_Effect: Sedation, dizziness, nausea, vomiting, constipation. Serious: Psychotomimetic effects (hallucinations, confusion), respiratory depression.
Dosage: Oral: 50 $\text{mg}$ three to four times daily. IM/IV/SC: 30 $\text{mg}$ to 60 $\text{mg}$ every 3-4 hours.

Symptom_1: Symptomatic treatment of chronic occlusive Peripheral Arterial Disease (PAD) (Intermittent Claudication)
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, dyspepsia, dizziness, headache.
Dosage: 400 $\text{mg}$ three times daily with meals.

Symptom_2: Adjunctive treatment of Primary Generalized Tonic-Clonic Seizures
Side_Effect: Dizziness, somnolence, fatigue, irritability, aggression. Serious: Behavioral and psychiatric adverse reactions ($\text{Black Box Warning}$).
Dosage: Initial: 2 $\text{mg}$ once daily at bedtime. Maintenance: 4 $\text{mg}$ to 12 $\text{mg}$ once daily.

Symptom_1: Management of acute and chronic Psychosis
Side_Effect: Sedation, dry mouth, blurred vision, orthostatic hypotension, extrapyramidal symptoms.
Dosage: Initial: 10 $\text{mg}$ to 30 $\text{mg}$ daily. Maintenance: 30 $\text{mg}$ to 150 $\text{mg}$ daily.

Symptom_2: Congestive Heart Failure, Stable Coronary Artery Disease
Side_Effect: Dry cough, dizziness, fatigue. Serious: Angioedema, hyperkalemia, renal impairment.
Dosage: 4 $\text{mg}$ to 8 $\text{mg}$ once daily.

Side_Effect: Local burning, stinging, itching, mild rash.
Dosage: Scabies (5% cream): Apply neck to soles, leave 8-14 hours, wash off (one application usually sufficient). Lice (1% lotion): Apply to hair/scalp, leave 10 minutes, rinse.

Symptom_1: Treatment of acute and chronic Psychosis (Schizophrenia)
Side_Effect: Sedation, dizziness, dry mouth, blurred vision. High risk of Extrapyramidal Symptoms ($\text{EPS}$) like tremor and tardive dyskinesia.
Dosage: Psychosis: 8 $\text{mg}$ to 16 $\text{mg}$ two to three times daily. Nausea: 2 $\text{mg}$ to 4 $\text{mg}$ three times daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of refractory Major Depressive Disorder (MDD)
Side_Effect: Orthostatic hypotension, dizziness, insomnia, weight gain, sexual dysfunction. Serious: Hypertensive Crisis (with tyramine-rich foods), hepatotoxicity, Serotonin Syndrome.
Dosage: 15 $\text{mg}$ three times daily (titrated slowly).

Symptom_1: Management of all forms of Epilepsy (except absence seizures)
Side_Effect: Drowsiness, sedation, ataxia, dependence, cognitive impairment. Serious: Respiratory depression, SJS/TEN (skin reactions).
Dosage: Epilepsy: 60 $\text{mg}$ to 200 $\text{mg}$ once daily. Insomnia: 100 $\text{mg}$ to 320 $\text{mg}$ at bedtime.

Symptom_1: Treatment of Hypertension and sweating associated with Phaeochromocytoma
Side_Effect: Orthostatic hypotension, dizziness, reflex tachycardia, nasal congestion, blurred vision.
Dosage: 10 $\text{mg}$ to 20 $\text{mg}$ two to three times daily (titrated slowly).

Side_Effect: Hypertension, headache, bradycardia, restlessness. Serious: Tissue necrosis (with extravasation).
Dosage: Nasal: 2-3 drops/sprays every 4 hours. IV: Bolus/infusion (titrated).

Symptom_1: Treatment of tonic-clonic and partial Seizures (Epilepsy)
Side_Effect: Gum hyperplasia, hirsutism, rash, coarse facial features, tremor, nystagmus. Serious: SJS/TEN, hepatotoxicity, $\text{Folate}$ deficiency, cardiac arrhythmias (IV).
Dosage: Oral: 300 $\text{mg}$ to 400 $\text{mg}$ daily (monitoring serum levels). IV: Loading dose for status.

Symptom_1: Symptomatic relief of persistent, unproductive Cough
Side_Effect: Sedation, dizziness, constipation, nausea. Warning: May increase risk of anaphylaxis to neuromuscular blocking agents.
Dosage: 5 $\text{mg}$ to 10 $\text{mg}$ three to four times daily.

Symptom_2: Treatment of Dry Mouth (Xerostomia) due to Sjogren's Syndrome or head/neck radiation
Side_Effect: Eye: Miosis, blurred vision, headache (brow ache). Oral: Sweating, flushing, nausea, increased salivation.
Dosage: Eye drops: 1-2 drops three to four times daily. Oral: 5 $\text{mg}$ to 10 $\text{mg}$ three times daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of severe Tics and vocal utterances associated with Tourette's Disorder
Side_Effect: $\text{EPS}$ (parkinsonism, dystonia), sedation, weight gain. Serious: QT prolongation (high risk, requires ECG monitoring) - Black Box Warning.
Dosage: Initial: 1 $\text{mg}$ to 2 $\text{mg}$ daily. Maintenance: up to 10 $\text{mg}$ daily.

Symptom_1: Management of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (improves glycemic control)
Side_Effect: Edema/fluid retention, weight gain, $\text{URI}$. Serious: Worsening Heart Failure ($\text{Black Box Warning}$), bladder cancer risk, bone fractures.
Dosage: 15 $\text{mg}$ to 45 $\text{mg}$ once daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of serious susceptible Bacterial Infections (e.g., Hospital-acquired Pneumonia, Sepsis, complicated $\text{UTI}$)
Side_Effect: Diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, rash. Serious: Hypersensitivity (anaphylaxis), $\text{C}$. difficile infection, Thrombocytopenia (high doses).
Price: Extremely High Cost; Hospital/IV use only
Dosage: IV infusion (3.375 $\text{g}$ to 4.5 $\text{g}$ every 6 to 8 hours).

Symptom_2: Cognitive enhancer (Nootropic) (unlicensed use in many areas)
Side_Effect: Nervousness, agitation, insomnia, weight gain, diarrhea.
Dosage: Myoclonus: 7.2 $\text{g}$ daily (in 2-3 doses), increased to 20 $\text{g}$ to 24 $\text{g}$ daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of mild to moderate Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis (IPF)
Side_Effect: Nausea, rash, photosensitivity, diarrhea, fatigue, liver enzyme elevation.
Dosage: Titrated up to 801 $\text{mg}$ three times daily (with food).

Symptom_1: Treatment of chronic Rheumatoid Arthritis and Osteoarthritis
Side_Effect: Dyspepsia, nausea, abdominal pain. Highest GI risk among NSAIDs (avoid long-term use, especially in elderly). Serious: GI bleeding, renal toxicity.
Dosage: 20 $\text{mg}$ once daily (Max 20 $\text{mg}$/day).

Side_Effect: Myalgia (muscle pain), headache, constipation, back pain. Lower drug interaction profile than other statins.
Dosage: 1 $\text{mg}$ to 4 $\text{mg}$ once daily.

Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, rash. Prodrug of Mecillinam.
Dosage: 400 $\text{mg}$ three times daily for 3-7 days.

Side_Effect: Bloating, gas, abdominal cramps. Requires adequate fluid intake.
Dosage: 1 $\text{g}$ (two tablets) four times daily (Max 6 $\text{g}$/day).

Symptom_1: Treatment of serious susceptible Gram-negative Bacterial Infections (MDR $\text{P}$. aeruginosa)
Symptom_2: Topical treatment of eye/ear/skin infections (in combination)
Side_Effect: Serious: Nephrotoxicity (dose-limiting), Neurotoxicity (paresthesia, confusion).
Dosage: IV infusion (Dosing complex, based on $\text{kg}$). Topical application varies.

Symptom_1: Treatment and prevention of Hypokalemia (Low Potassium)
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal discomfort. Serious: Hyperkalemia (high potassium, fatal arrhythmias), GI ulceration (especially solid oral forms).
Dosage: Highly individualized (e.g., 20 $\text{mEq}$ to 80 $\text{mEq}$ daily). Must be diluted/slowly infused (IV) or taken with food (Oral).

Side_Effect: Headache, GI upset, muscle pain (myalgia). Hydrophilic (lower $\text{CNS}$ side effects).
Dosage: 10 $\text{mg}$ to 40 $\text{mg}$ once daily (Max 80 $\text{mg}$/day).

Side_Effect: First-Dose Phenomenon (severe orthostatic hypotension), dizziness, headache, fatigue.
Dosage: Initial: 1 $\text{mg}$ at bedtime. Maintenance: 2 $\text{mg}$ to 20 $\text{mg}$ daily (in 2-3 doses).

Symptom_1: Management of acute severe Inflammation (e.g., acute asthma, allergies)
Side_Effect: Insomnia, mood changes, increased appetite, fluid retention. Long-term: Cushingoid features, osteoporosis, infection risk, hyperglycemia.
Dosage: Dose varies widely (often 5 $\text{mg}$ to 60 $\text{mg}$ daily). Requires tapering for prolonged use.

Symptom_1: Treatment of Neuropathic Pain (Diabetic neuropathy, Post-herpetic neuralgia)
Symptom_2: Adjunctive therapy for Partial-Onset Seizures, Fibromyalgia
Side_Effect: Dizziness, somnolence, peripheral edema, weight gain, blurred vision. Serious: Angioedema, respiratory depression (when combined with opioids).
Dosage: 75 $\text{mg}$ to 300 $\text{mg}$ twice daily (Max 600 $\text{mg}$/day).

Symptom_1: Treatment of generalized tonic-clonic and partial Seizures (Epilepsy)
Side_Effect: Sedation, dizziness, ataxia, cognitive impairment, $\text{GI}$ upset. Metabolized to Phenobarbital.
Dosage: Initial: 50 $\text{mg}$ to 125 $\text{mg}$ at bedtime. Maintenance: 750 $\text{mg}$ to 1500 $\text{mg}$ daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of severe Nausea and Vomiting
Symptom_2: Management of acute and chronic Psychosis
Side_Effect: Sedation, dizziness, dry mouth. Risk of $\text{EPS}$ and $\text{Tardive Dyskinesia}$.
Dosage: Nausea: 5 $\text{mg}$ to 10 $\text{mg}$ three to four times daily. Psychosis: up to 150 $\text{mg}$ daily.

Symptom_2: Sedation/Sleep aid, Treatment/Prophylaxis of Motion Sickness
Side_Effect: Marked drowsiness/sedation, dizziness, dry mouth, blurred vision. Contraindicated in children under 2 years ($\text{fatal respiratory depression}$).
Dosage: Allergy: 25 $\text{mg}$ at night. Motion Sickness: 25 $\text{mg}$ 30-60 mins before travel.

Symptom_1: Treatment of various Supraventricular and Ventricular Arrhythmias
Side_Effect: Dizziness, metallic/bitter taste, nausea, constipation, tremor. Serious: Pro-arrhythmic effects (new/worse arrhythmias), heart failure exacerbation.
Dosage: 150 $\text{mg}$ every 8 hours (titrated up to 300 $\text{mg}$ every 8 hours).

Symptom_1: Induction and maintenance of general Anesthesia
Symptom_2: Sedation for mechanically ventilated patients in $\text{ICU}$
Side_Effect: Hypotension, injection site pain, apnea, bradycardia. Serious: Propofol Infusion Syndrome ($\text{PRIS}$) (metabolic acidosis, rhabdomyolysis, renal failure).
Dosage: IV bolus for induction (1.5 $\text{mg}/\text{kg}$ to 2.5 $\text{mg}/\text{kg}$), then continuous IV infusion.

Symptom_2: Preferred agent during the first trimester of pregnancy for hyperthyroidism
Side_Effect: Rash, nausea, taste disturbance, joint pain. Serious: Severe Hepatotoxicity/Failure ($\text{Black Box Warning}$), agranulocytosis (low white blood cells).
Dosage: Initial: 50 $\text{mg}$ to 150 $\text{mg}$ every 8 hours. Maintenance: 50 $\text{mg}$ three times daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of Hypertension and Angina Pectoris
Symptom_2: Prophylaxis of Migraine, Essential Tremor, Performance Anxiety
Side_Effect: Bradycardia, dizziness, fatigue, cold extremities. Serious: Bronchospasm (contraindicated in asthma), worsening heart failure, masking of hypoglycemia symptoms.
Dosage: 40 $\text{mg}$ to 160 $\text{mg}$ twice daily (IR) or once daily (LA).

Symptom_1: Preservative in medications, cosmetics, and foods
Side_Effect: Allergic contact dermatitis, rash. Generally recognized as safe in low concentrations.
Dosage: Used as an ingredient in various formulations; typically $\le$ 0.1% concentration.

Symptom_1: Rapid reversal of Warfarin (Vitamin K antagonist) anticoagulant effects
Symptom_2: Treatment of bleeding in patients with congenital clotting factor deficiencies ($\text{II}, \text{VII}, \text{IX}, \text{X}$)
Side_Effect: Headache, dizziness, rash. Serious: Thromboembolism (risk of stroke, $\text{DVT}$/$\text{PE}$), allergic reactions.
Price: Extremely High Cost; Hospital/IV use only
Dosage: IV infusion (Dose based on factor activity, patient weight, and $\text{INR}$).

Symptom_1: Treatment of chronic Idiopathic Constipation (when other laxatives fail)
Side_Effect: Headache, nausea, abdominal pain, diarrhea.
Dosage: 2 $\text{mg}$ once daily (1 $\text{mg}$ in elderly or with renal impairment).

Symptom_2: Irritable Bowel Syndrome ($\text{IBS}$), Cholesterol lowering (as supplement)
Side_Effect: Bloating, gas, abdominal discomfort. Requires adequate fluid intake.
Dosage: 5 $\text{g}$ to 10 $\text{g}$ mixed with water, one to three times daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of active Tuberculosis (TB) (part of a multi-drug regimen)
Side_Effect: Arthralgia (joint pain), $\text{GI}$ upset, hyperuricemia (gout risk). Serious: Hepatotoxicity (requires monitoring), severe skin reactions.
Dosage: 15 $\text{mg}/\text{kg}$ to 30 $\text{mg}/\text{kg}$ daily (Max 2 $\text{g}$/day).

Side_Effect: Cholinergic effects: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal cramps, increased salivation, bradycardia.
Dosage: 60 $\text{mg}$ to 150 $\text{mg}$ three to six times daily (titrated to effect).

Symptom_1: Prevention and treatment of Isoniazid-induced Peripheral Neuropathy
Symptom_2: Treatment of $\text{B}_6$ deficiency, Management of Nausea in Pregnancy (in combination)
Side_Effect: Sensory neuropathy (high dose/long-term), headache.
Dosage: Isoniazid Prophylaxis: 10 $\text{mg}$ to 50 $\text{mg}$ daily. Deficiency: Varies.

Symptom_1: Treatment of acute and chronic Schizophrenia
Symptom_2: Treatment of acute Mania and Bipolar Depression
Side_Effect: Somnolence, dry mouth, dizziness, weight gain, increased blood lipids/glucose. Serious: $\text{QT}$ prolongation, $\text{NMS}$, $\text{TD}$.
Dosage: Schizophrenia: 400 $\text{mg}$ to 800 $\text{mg}$ daily. Depression: 50 $\text{mg}$ to 300 $\text{mg}$ daily.

Side_Effect: Dry cough, dizziness, fatigue. Serious: Angioedema, hyperkalemia, renal impairment.
Dosage: 10 $\text{mg}$ to 40 $\text{mg}$ once daily or in two divided doses.

Symptom_1: Treatment of various Supraventricular and Ventricular Arrhythmias
Side_Effect: Diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, Cinchonism (tinnitus, headache, visual disturbance). Serious: Torsades de Pointes ($\text{QT}$ prolongation), Pro-arrhythmic effects, thrombocytopenia.
Dosage: 200 $\text{mg}$ to 400 $\text{mg}$ three to four times daily (Oral).

Symptom_1: Treatment of uncomplicated Malaria (due to $\text{P}$. falciparum)
Symptom_2: Treatment of nocturnal Leg Cramps (rarely used, high risk)
Side_Effect: Cinchonism (tinnitus, headache, visual disturbance), nausea, vomiting. Serious: $\text{QT}$ prolongation, Hemolysis, Thrombocytopenia.
Dosage: Malaria: 600 $\text{mg}$ three times daily for 7 days.

Symptom_2: Treatment of Peptic Ulcers, $\text{H}$. pylori eradication (in combination)
Side_Effect: Headache, diarrhea, nausea, pharyngitis.
Dosage: 20 $\text{mg}$ once daily, 30 minutes before food.

Symptom_2: Prevention of cardiovascular events, Congestive Heart Failure
Side_Effect: Dry cough, dizziness, fatigue. Serious: Angioedema, hyperkalemia, renal impairment.
Dosage: 2.5 $\text{mg}$ to 10 $\text{mg}$ once daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of Duodenal and Gastric Ulcers
Side_Effect: Headache, dizziness, constipation, diarrhea. (Use restricted in many countries due to $\text{NDMA}$ impurity concern).
Dosage: 150 $\text{mg}$ twice daily or 300 $\text{mg}$ at bedtime.

Symptom_1: Treatment of chronic stable Angina Pectoris
Side_Effect: Dizziness, headache, constipation, nausea. Serious: $\text{QT}$ prolongation (dose-dependent).
Dosage: 500 $\text{mg}$ to 1000 $\text{mg}$ twice daily.

Side_Effect: Fatigue, $\text{HFS}$ (Hand-Foot Skin Reaction), diarrhea, hypertension. Serious: Hepatotoxicity ($\text{Black Box Warning}$), hemorrhage, $\text{GI}$ perforation.
Price: Extremely High Cost; Oncology use only
Dosage: 160 $\text{mg}$ once daily for 21 days, followed by 7 days off (28-day cycle).

Symptom_1: Short-acting opioid for General Anesthesia and procedural sedation
Side_Effect: Respiratory depression, bradycardia, muscle rigidity, hypotension. Extremely rapid onset and offset (metabolized by blood/tissue esterases).
Price: Extremely High Cost; Hospital/IV use only
Dosage: IV infusion (Dosing highly individualized, based on $\text{mcg}/\text{kg}/\text{min}$).

Side_Effect: Hypoglycemia (low blood sugar), headache, $\text{URI}$, joint pain. Taken immediately before meals to mimic physiological insulin release.
Dosage: 0.5 $\text{mg}$ to 4 $\text{mg}$ taken 15-30 minutes before each meal.

Side_Effect: Bleeding (major complication), headache, nausea, hypotension.
Price: Extremely High Cost; Emergency IV use only
Dosage: Double IV bolus dose (10 $\text{MU}$ bolus, followed by 10 $\text{MU}$ 30 minutes later).

Symptom_1: Treatment of active Tuberculosis (TB) (part of a multi-drug regimen)
Side_Effect: Red-orange discoloration of all body fluids (urine, sweat, tears), $\text{GI}$ upset. Serious: Hepatotoxicity, severe skin reactions. Potent enzyme inducer (many drug interactions).
Dosage: 10 $\text{mg}/\text{kg}$ daily (Max 600 $\text{mg}$/day).

Symptom_1: Treatment of Traveler's Diarrhea (due to non-invasive $\text{E}$. coli)
Symptom_2: Reduction of risk of Overt Hepatic Encephalopathy recurrence
Side_Effect: Flatulence, headache, abdominal pain, peripheral edema. Poorly absorbed (works primarily in the gut).
Dosage: Traveler's Diarrhea: 200 $\text{mg}$ three times daily for 3 days. $\text{HE}$: 550 $\text{mg}$ twice daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis ($\text{ALS}$) (Slightly extends survival/delays ventilation)
Side_Effect: Asthenia (weakness), nausea, dizziness, $\text{GI}$ upset. Serious: Hepatotoxicity (requires liver enzyme monitoring), neutropenia.
Dosage: 50 $\text{mg}$ twice daily.

Symptom_2: Treatment of Paget's Disease of Bone
Side_Effect: $\text{GI}$ upset, dyspepsia, abdominal pain. Serious: Esophageal irritation/ulceration (requires sitting upright after dose), $\text{ONJ}$, atypical femur fractures.
Dosage: 35 $\text{mg}$ once weekly or 150 $\text{mg}$ once monthly.

Symptom_1: Treatment of acute and chronic Schizophrenia
Symptom_2: Treatment of acute Mania in Bipolar Disorder, Irritability associated with Autism
Side_Effect: Insomnia, sedation, headache, $\text{EPS}$ (dose-dependent). Highest risk of hyperprolactinemia among atypical antipsychotics.
Dosage: 2 $\text{mg}$ to 6 $\text{mg}$ daily (divided or single dose). Long-acting injection available.

Symptom_1: Boosting (Pharmacokinetic enhancement) of other Protease Inhibitors ($\text{PI}$)
Symptom_2: Treatment of $\text{HIV-1}$ Infection (rarely used as sole $\text{PI}$)
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal pain, paresthesia (mouth/feet). Serious: Hepatotoxicity, significant metabolic disturbances.
Dosage: Boosting dose: 100 $\text{mg}$ to 200 $\text{mg}$ once or twice daily.

Side_Effect: $\text{Infusion}$ reactions (fever, chills, rash, angioedema), headache. Serious: $\text{PML}$ ($\text{Black Box Warning}$), hepatitis $\text{B}$ reactivation.
Price: Extremely High Cost; Oncology IV Infusion
Dosage: $\text{NHL}$: 375 $\text{mg}/\text{m}^2$ IV weekly (x4 cycles). $\text{RA}$: 1 $\text{g}$ IV $\times$ 2 doses (separated by 2 weeks) every 6-9 months.

Symptom_1: Prevention of stroke/systemic embolism in non-valvular Atrial Fibrillation ($\text{AF}$)
Symptom_2: Prevention/Treatment of $\text{DVT}$/$\text{PE}$, Prophylaxis after hip/knee replacement
Side_Effect: Bleeding (major complication), nausea, abdominal pain. Serious: Spinal/Epidural hematoma ($\text{Black Box Warning}$ with neuraxial procedures).
Dosage: $\text{AF}$: 20 $\text{mg}$ once daily. $\text{DVT}/\text{PE}$: Initial 15 $\text{mg}$ twice daily, then 20 $\text{mg}$ once daily.

Symptom_1: Acute treatment of Migraine attacks (with or without aura)
Side_Effect: Dizziness, somnolence, fatigue, nausea, dry mouth. Rapid onset of action.
Dosage: 5 $\text{mg}$ to 10 $\text{mg}$ at onset (may repeat once after 2 hours).

Symptom_1: Treatment of moderate to severe Restless Legs Syndrome ($\text{RLS}$)
Symptom_2: Treatment of symptoms of Parkinson's Disease
Side_Effect: Somnolence, dizziness, nausea, vomiting, sudden onset of sleep. Serious: Compulsive behaviors (gambling, hypersexuality), orthostatic hypotension.
Dosage: $\text{RLS}$: 0.25 $\text{mg}$ to 4 $\text{mg}$ once daily (at bedtime). $\text{PD}$: Slowly titrated up to 24 $\text{mg}$ daily.

Side_Effect: Belching, flatulence, fluid retention, metabolic alkalosis (overdose).
Dosage: Antacid: 325 $\text{mg}$ to 2 $\text{g}$ one to four times daily. $\text{IV}$: Based on severity of acidosis.

Symptom_1: Treatment of generalized and partial Seizures (Epilepsy)
Symptom_2: Treatment of acute Mania in Bipolar Disorder, Prophylaxis of Migraine
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, tremor, weight gain, hair loss. Serious: Hepatotoxicity ($\text{Black Box Warning}$, especially children < 2 yrs), Pancreatitis, neural tube defects (Teratogenic).
Dosage: Highly individualized; 15 $\text{mg}/\text{kg}/\text{day}$ to 60 $\text{mg}/\text{kg}/\text{day}$ (divided doses or $\text{ER}$).

Symptom_1: Treatment of Overactive Bladder ($\text{OAB}$) (Urinary frequency, urgency, incontinence)
Side_Effect: Dry mouth, constipation, blurred vision, dizziness. Serious: Urinary retention, $\text{CNS}$ effects (in elderly).
Dosage: 5 $\text{mg}$ to 10 $\text{mg}$ once daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of Growth Hormone Deficiency (GHD) in children and adults
Symptom_2: Treatment of short stature due to various causes (e.g., Turner Syndrome, $\text{CKD}$)
Side_Effect: Injection site reactions, headache, hyperglycemia. Serious: Increased risk of $\text{Slipped Capital Femoral Epiphysis}$ in children, $\text{Intracranial Hypertension}$.
Dosage: SC injection (Dosing complex, based on weight/surface area/indication).

Side_Effect: Diarrhea, abdominal cramps, flatulence (excessive consumption).
Dosage: 30 $\text{mL}$ to 150 $\text{mL}$ of 70% solution (as needed).

Symptom_2: Maintenance of sinus rhythm in $\text{Atrial Fibrillation/Flutter}$
Side_Effect: Bradycardia, fatigue, dizziness, hypotension. Serious: Torsades de Pointes ($\text{QT}$ prolongation) - $\text{Black Box Warning}$ (must be initiated in hospital).
Uses: Beta-Blocker ($\text{Class II}$) + Potassium Channel Blocker ($\text{Class III}$)
Dosage: Initial: 80 $\text{mg}$ twice daily. Maintenance: 160 $\text{mg}$ to 320 $\text{mg}$ daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of Hypertension and Congestive Heart Failure
Side_Effect: Hyperkalemia, gynecomastia (men), breast tenderness (women), $\text{GI}$ upset. Serious: Acute renal failure, $\text{SJS}$.
Dosage: 25 $\text{mg}$ to 100 $\text{mg}$ once daily (Max 400 $\text{mg}$).

Symptom_1: Treatment of HIV-1 Infection (less used due to toxicity)
Side_Effect: Peripheral neuropathy, lipodystrophy (fat wasting). Serious: Lactic Acidosis/Severe Hepatomegaly with Steatosis ($\text{Black Box Warning}$).
Dosage: 30 $\text{mg}$ to 40 $\text{mg}$ twice daily.

Symptom_2: Short-term maintenance of Duodenal Ulcer healing, $\text{GERD}$/Esophagitis
Side_Effect: Constipation, dry mouth. Requires acidic environment for activation (separate from $\text{PPI}$/antacids).
Dosage: 1 $\text{g}$ four times daily (on an empty stomach).

Symptom_1: Treatment of Ulcerative Colitis (UC) and Crohn's Disease
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, headache, $\text{GI}$ upset, yellow-orange skin/urine discoloration. Serious: Myelosuppression, hepatotoxicity, kidney stone formation.
Dosage: $\text{UC}/\text{CD}$: 1 $\text{g}$ to 2 $\text{g}$ two to three times daily. $\text{RA}$: 500 $\text{mg}$ to 1000 $\text{mg}$ twice daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of various susceptible Bacterial Infections
Symptom_2: Prophylaxis of Rheumatic Fever, Toxoplasmosis (in combination)
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, rash. Serious: $\text{SJS}/\text{TEN}$, Hemolytic anemia, Crystalluria (kidney stone risk).
Dosage: Varies by indication (often 1 $\text{g}$ to 2 $\text{g}$ daily in divided doses).

Symptom_1: Treatment of various susceptible Bacterial Infections (e.g., $\text{UTI}$, $\text{PCP}$)
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, rash, increased serum potassium. Serious: SJS/TEN ($\text{Black Box Warning}$), Myelosuppression, renal failure.
Dosage: $\text{UTI}$: 1 DS tab twice daily. $\text{PCP}$: High doses based on $\text{kg}$.

Symptom_1: Acute treatment of Migraine attacks (with or without aura)
Side_Effect: Chest tightness/pressure (non-cardiac), dizziness, tingling, flushing.
Dosage: Oral: 25 $\text{mg}$ to 100 $\text{mg}$ at onset (may repeat after 2 hours). $\text{SC}$: 6 $\text{mg}$ dose.

Symptom_1: Treatment of advanced Renal Cell Carcinoma (RCC)
Side_Effect: Fatigue, diarrhea, nausea, $\text{HFS}$ (Hand-Foot Skin Reaction), hypertension, skin discoloration (yellow). Serious: Hepatotoxicity, $\text{QT}$ prolongation, thyroid dysfunction.
Price: Extremely High Cost; Oncology use only
Dosage: 50 $\text{mg}$ once daily for 4 weeks, followed by 2 weeks off (6-week cycle).

Symptom_1: Prevention of rejection in solid organ Transplantation (Kidney, Liver, Heart)
Side_Effect: Nephrotoxicity (dose-limiting), tremor, headache, hypertension, hyperglycemia. Serious: Increased risk of infection/malignancy. Requires $\text{TDM}$.
Dosage: Oral: Highly individualized (based on $\text{TDM}$). Topical: Apply two to three times daily.

Symptom_2: Treatment of Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension ($\text{PAH}$), Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia ($\text{BPH}$)
Side_Effect: Headache, flushing, dyspepsia, back pain/myalgia. Serious: Sudden hearing/vision loss, Priapism, severe hypotension (contraindicated with Nitrates). Longest duration of action.
Dosage: ED: 10 $\text{mg}$ to 20 $\text{mg}$ as needed, or 2.5 $\text{mg}$ to 5 $\text{mg}$ daily. $\text{PAH}$: 40 $\text{mg}$ once daily.

Symptom_1: Adjuvant and metastatic Breast Cancer ($\text{ER}$+ positive)
Symptom_2: Prevention of Breast Cancer in high-risk women
Side_Effect: Hot flashes, vaginal discharge/bleeding, nausea. Serious: Endometrial cancer, Uterine sarcoma, Thromboembolism ($\text{DVT}/\text{PE}$) - Black Box Warning.
Dosage: 20 $\text{mg}$ once daily for 5-10 years.

Symptom_1: Symptomatic treatment of Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia ($\text{BPH}$)
Side_Effect: Dizziness, headache, abnormal ejaculation (reduced/absent), orthostatic hypotension. Serious: Intraoperative Floppy Iris Syndrome ($\text{IFIS}$) during cataract surgery.
Dosage: 0.4 $\text{mg}$ once daily (30 minutes after the same meal each day).

Symptom_1: Management of moderate to severe acute and chronic Pain
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, dizziness, somnolence, constipation. Serious: Respiratory Depression, Serotonin Syndrome, seizure risk, dependence/abuse.
Dosage: IR: 50 $\text{mg}$ to 100 $\text{mg}$ every 4-6 hours. $\text{ER}$: 50 $\text{mg}$ to 250 $\text{mg}$ twice daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of chronic Hepatitis B Virus ($\text{HBV}$) infection
Symptom_2: Treatment of HIV-1 Infection (part of combination therapy)
Side_Effect: TDF: Renal impairment (Fanconi syndrome), decreased bone mineral density. TAF: Less bone/renal toxicity, increased lipids.
Dosage: $\text{HBV/HIV}$: 300 $\text{mg}$ (TDF) or 25 $\text{mg}$ (TAF) once daily.

Symptom_2: Symptomatic treatment of Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia ($\text{BPH}$)
Side_Effect: Dizziness, headache, lethargy, orthostatic hypotension ($\text{First-Dose}$ Phenomenon).
Dosage: Initial: 1 $\text{mg}$ at bedtime. Maintenance: 1 $\text{mg}$ to 20 $\text{mg}$ once daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of Onychomycosis (Fungal infection of nails)
Symptom_2: Treatment of Tinea infections (Athlete's foot, ringworm)
Side_Effect: Headache, $\text{GI}$ upset, rash, taste disturbance (can be prolonged). Serious: Hepatotoxicity (requires liver function monitoring).
Dosage: Onychomycosis: 250 $\text{mg}$ once daily (6 weeks for fingernails, 12 weeks for toenails).

Symptom_2: Tocolysis (relaxation of uterus) for premature labor (IV/SC - often discouraged)
Side_Effect: Tachycardia, tremor, nervousness, headache, hypokalemia.
Dosage: SC Injection: 0.25 $\text{mg}$ repeated once. Oral: 2.5 $\text{mg}$ to 5 $\text{mg}$ three times daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of severe Osteoporosis (increases bone density and strength)
Side_Effect: Nausea, headache, dizziness, injection site pain/reaction. Serious: Osteosarcoma ($\text{Black Box Warning}$ - seen in rats), hypercalcemia. Use limited to 2 years lifetime.
Price: Extremely High Cost; Specialty SC Injection
Dosage: 20 $\mu\text{g}$ SC once daily.

Symptom_1: Treatment of primary or secondary Hypogonadism (low testosterone) in men
Side_Effect: Acne, oily skin, gynecomastia, fluid retention, polycythemia (high $\text{RBC}$ count). Serious: Increased cardiovascular risk (observed in some studies), prostate cancer progression.
Price: Varies widely (Gel: Very High Cost; Injection: Moderate)
Dosage: Varies by formulation ($\text{IM}$ injection every 2-4 weeks, $\text{Gel}$ daily, $\text{Patch}$ daily).

Symptom_1: Treatment of various susceptible Bacterial Infections (e.g., Acne, Respiratory, $\text{GU}$)
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, photosensitivity. Serious: Permanent tooth discoloration (children < 8), pseudotumor cerebri.
Dosage: 250 $\text{mg}$ to 500 $\text{mg}$ four times daily (take on empty stomach, avoid dairy/antacids).

Symptom_1: Treatment of newly diagnosed Multiple Myeloma (in combination)
Side_Effect: Somnolence, dizziness, constipation, peripheral neuropathy. Serious: Severe Teratogenicity (Phocomelia) - $\text{Black Box Warning}$ (strict $\text{REMS}$ program required), $\text{VTE}$ (venous thromboembolism).
Dosage: 100 $\text{mg}$ to 400 $\text{mg}$ daily (dosing varies by indication).

Symptom_1: Maintenance treatment of Asthma and COPD
Side_Effect: Nausea, vomiting, headache, restlessness, insomnia, tremor. Serious: Toxicity (seizures, cardiac arrhythmias), especially with narrow therapeutic index (requires monitoring).
Dosage: Highly individualized (dosing based on $\text{mg}/\text{kg}$ and serum levels). $\text{ER}$: Once or twice daily.

Side_Effect: Myelosuppression, nausea, vomiting, hepatotoxicity. Serious: Veno-occlusive disease of the liver (VOD) (especially high-dose), bone marrow suppression.
Price: Very High Cost; Oncology use only
Dosage: 2 $\text{mg}/\text{kg}$ daily or 80 $\text{mg}$ to 100 $\text{mg}/\text{m}^2$ daily.

Side_Effect: Sedation, dry mouth, blurred vision, orthostatic hypotension. Serious: QT prolongation/Torsades de Pointes ($\text{Black Box Warning}$) - should be reserved for treatment failures, Retinal Pigment Change.
Dosage: 100 $\text{mg}$ to 600 $\text{mg}$ daily (in divided doses).

Symptom_1: Treatment of acute and chronic Schizophrenia
Side_Effect: $\text{EPS}$ (dystonia, akathisia), sedation, orthostatic hypotension. Serious: $\text{QT}$ prolongation, $\text{NMS}$, $\text{TD}$.
Dosage: 2 $\text{mg}$ to 5 $\text{mg}$ two to three times daily. Maintenance: up to 60 $\text{mg}$ daily.
